fix: competitor intercept varchar(255) overflow — constrain LLM output fields

winner and winning_factor from GPT could exceed the DB varchar(255) limit,
causing "value too long" errors on Run Analysis. Added .max(255) to Zod
schema + .slice(0, 255) safety net at insert site.

commit.message:
Add comprehensive run diagnostics + grace period on req.close kill

- Pre-spawn su sanity test shows id/uid before each run
- Script echoes 'Running as: $(id)' and 'claude exited: $?'
- PID and spawn timing logged to terminal
- stderr from su/bash logged to Railway console + terminal
- elapsed time shown on close (code, signal, ms)
- req.on('close') now has 10s grace period to avoid killing on
  Railway proxy SSE reconnects (likely root cause of SIGTERM)
- Railway console logs for req.close and proc.close events

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ops: recreate ansible vault, unblock VAULT deployments

- Recreate secrets.vault.yml encrypted with correct password
  (recovered secrets from git history + running containers)
- Install paramiko into ansible-core venv for VAULT SSH
- Verify: ansible vault -m ping returns SUCCESS
- Update STATUS.md and SESSION-LOG.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: comprehensive audit — 6 critical bug fixes across codebase

1. Discover route: fix null pageId collision in enrichment matching
   (match by pageId + pageName to avoid wrong data for null pageIds)

2. Worker: wrap landing page delete+save in $transaction for atomicity
   (prevents data loss if save fails after delete)

3. recalculateAllAdStatuses: cursor-based pagination instead of loading
   ALL ads into memory (prevents OOM on large datasets)

4. Platforms route: add console.error logging for course/platform errors
   (was silently swallowing all errors making debugging impossible)

5. Puppeteer: proper cleanup of disconnected browser instances
   (try/catch around close of dead browsers, prevent zombie processes)

6. Settings: try-catch around decrypt for missing APP_ENCRYPTION_KEY
   (returns null instead of crashing the entire request)

feat: video play/pause toggle with flash indicator

Click on video to pause/play. Shows a translucent blur circle with
pause (||) or play (▶) icon that flashes for 600ms on each toggle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Optimize streaming speed: stream segments while downloading, tune hls.js

- proxy: stream segment bytes to client simultaneously with caching
  (cuts TTFB from full-segment-duration to near-zero on slow CDNs)
- proxy: LRU cache 300→500, prefetch 8→12 segments, prefetch 1→3 variants
- hls.js: liveSyncDurationCount=2 (closer to live edge), tighter starvation
  delays (2s), faster buffer watchdog (500ms), smaller backBuffer (10s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): use PAT for release-please and update manifest version

Org settings block GITHUB_TOKEN from creating PRs. Use a PAT via
RELEASE_PLEASE_TOKEN secret instead. Also update manifest to 0.1.1
to match the current published version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(refinery): skip MRs with missing branches instead of fatal exit (rc-d46) (#2539)

When a polecat's remote branch is cleaned up before the refinery
processes it (e.g. cherry-picked to target), doMerge returned a plain
error that processSingleMR treated as a fatal infrastructure failure,
halting the entire patrol queue.

Changes:
- Add BranchNotFound bool to ProcessResult to distinguish from other failures
- processSingleMR: treat BranchNotFound as a skip (Conflicts) not a fatal Error
- HandleMRInfoFailure: skip polecat nudge for BranchNotFound (polecat is gone)
- Formula process-branch: add Step 0.5 to verify branch still exists before
  attempting checkout, with explicit skip path (race condition guard)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Polish profile, help, my-goals, subscription screens and fix cross-screen consistency

- Profile: extract callbacks for memo effectiveness, add empty state recovery button,
  fix WCAG colors (emptySubtitle, userEmail, btnRestore, signOut), chevron contrast,
  restore button border, email accessibilityState
- Help: reorder FAQs, add accuracy/weigh-in FAQs, simplify calorie FAQ, fix colors
- My Goals: add empty state, discard-changes confirm, summary preview card, gain speed
  labels aligned with onboarding, inline validation, accessibility improvements
- Subscription: fix expiry logic with date guard, stub screen polish
- Edit Profile: fix activityDesc and emptySubtitle WCAG colors
- Units: fix emptySubtitle WCAG color
- Goal Speed: align gain labels with my-goals screen
- Auth: sign-out resets all stores except onboarding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: rename button label to "Generar con IA"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move gen: lazy idx computation, u&=u-1 bit clear, magic bitboards

- Defer bit_to_idx() calls inside update branches (only computed when
  a piece actually needs regenerating, not for every piece every call)
- Replace u ^= (U64)1<<idx with u &= u-1 in move encoding loops
- Implement magic bitboards for rook/bishop/queen attack generation:
  replaces prune_blocked_moves ray-walking with a single multiply+shift
  table lookup per slider piece. Magic numbers found at startup.

Perft: 10/10 PASS, ~341M NPS (up from ~290M before this session)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add traceability.json generation instruction to review prompt (audit wave 2)

Phase 5 of the review prompt now instructs the review agent to persist
AC pass/fail results as .automaton/traceability.json with a structured
schema (id, status, evidence). This makes traceability auditable and
machine-readable, enabling automated regression detection across review
iterations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix app crash when master_variable_list.csv is missing (e.g. Docker)

The docs/ directory was removed from git as proprietary data, so the
Docker container has no master_variable_list.csv. The dropdown builder
now catches FileNotFoundError and falls back to TLA-only labels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add web search capability to phone agent

Use Anthropic's built-in web_search tool so the agent can look up
information during calls. No extra API keys or dependencies needed —
the search runs server-side via the Anthropic API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add fail-closed pipeline, service auth, CSRF, supply chain pinning, and CI hardening (M26)

Phase 1 — Fail-closed quarantine pipeline:
- Remote artifacts without pinned hashes now hard-fail (Stage 3)
- Missing modelscan hard-fails when require_scan is true (Stage 5)
- Scanner versions (modelscan, cosign, llama-server) recorded in all stage results
- Watcher sends source URL, source revision, scanner versions, and policy hash to registry on promotion
- Model catalog entries gain expected_sha256 and expected_size_bytes fields
- Download path validates size/hash and restricts git-clone to allowlisted sources

Phase 2 — Localhost binding & service-to-service auth:
- Registry, tool-firewall, airlock default bind changed from 0.0.0.0 to 127.0.0.1
- Bootstrap service token generated at first boot (/run/secure-ai/service-token)
- Mutating endpoints (promote, delete, reload) require Bearer token; read-only endpoints stay open
- Graceful degradation: no token file = dev mode (no auth enforced)

Phase 3 — CSRF & session hardening:
- Double-submit cookie CSRF protection on all state-changing routes
- Transparent fetch() patching in base.html auto-includes X-CSRF-Token header
- Exemptions for service-to-service calls (valid Bearer token), pre-auth routes, and first-boot
- Session regeneration on login, secret key rotation on passphrase change (invalidates all sessions)
- Re-auth required for emergency panic level 2+

Phase 5 — Supply chain pinning:
- All Containerfile base images digest-pinned (python, golang, alpine)
- Python dependencies locked with pip-compile --generate-hashes
- Recipe base image pinning guidance added
- Container pin and action pin verification scripts

Phase 6 — CI/CD hardening:
- All GitHub Actions SHA-pinned with version comments
- CodeQL scanning workflow for Go and Python
- SBOM generation (CycloneDX) and cosign attestation in build workflow
- Pin enforcement CI job

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: validate guildId in button handlers to prevent cross-guild privilege escalation

Without this check, a user could craft a button interaction in Guild B
that acts on an elevation from Guild A, using Guild A's admin config to
pass the isWatchtowerAdmin check. All three handlers now reject interactions
where interaction.guildId !== elevation.guildId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Removed Manufacturers from sub-nav, hub category cards, and stats.

Gallery tables were missing from TABLE_CREATION_ORDER in
tableCreationOrder.mjs, preventing production sync from creating them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: align SGA labels with Oxy platform naming

- 4.20: full name "Retenções PIS/COFINS/CSLL de Serviços de Terceiros"
- 4.21: reordered (was after 4.22/4.23)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make expert delegation async to avoid blocking audio relay

askExpert() was called synchronously in processGeminiMessage,
blocking the Gemini→Client goroutine for up to 30s. During
that time no audio or transcripts could be relayed, causing
a disruptive pause. Now runs in a goroutine so Gemini can
continue streaming audio (e.g. "let me check on that") while
the expert round-trip completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: cascade delete thread replies and fix thread reaction updates (#316)

* fix: cascade delete thread replies and fix thread reaction updates

Deleting a message with thread replies caused a P2003 foreign key
constraint error. Now the backend cascade-deletes thread replies,
their attachments, and thread subscribers in application code before
deleting the parent message. The frontend shows a confirmation dialog
warning users about the thread deletion.

Also fixes thread reply reactions not updating in real-time by adding
parentMessageId to reaction WebSocket payloads and updating the
frontend handlers to also update the thread replies cache.

Closes #294

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: order pinned messages oldest-first

Pinned messages were ordered by pinnedAt desc (most recently pinned
first), making the display seem random. Changed to pinnedAt asc so
the oldest pins appear at the top.

Closes #296

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback

- Deduplicate handleConfirmThreadDelete by delegating to handleConfirmDelete
- Add cancelQueries before thread cache setQueryData in reaction handlers
- Remove redundant threadSubscriber.deleteMany (onDelete: Cascade handles it)
- Order pinned messages by pinnedAt asc (oldest pins first)

Closes #296

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Remember window size and state across sessions (#39)

* Remember window size and maximized state across sessions

Saves width, height, and maximized flag to settings.toml on window
close. Restores on next launch. Size is only saved when not
maximized/fullscreen (like Meld). Closes #9.

* Also remember fullscreen state across sessions

Unit tests: defaults, roundtrip, backward compat with old config.
UI test: verifies settings.toml contains window dimensions after close.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix clippy field_reassign_with_default in test

Use struct init syntax instead of default + reassign to satisfy
clippy -D warnings on CI (Rust 1.94).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: save-on-close ordering, sentinel defaults, hermetic test

- Only persist window state when close actually proceeds (not cancelled)
- Use 0 as sentinel for window_width/height so per-window defaults apply
  until user has actually resized (VCS=700x500, merge=1200x600, etc.)
- UI test uses tmp_path for XDG_CONFIG_HOME instead of real user config
- Assert process exited before checking settings file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/fix product image loading tgx4 h (#31) * Fix product image loading: add skeleton, retry logic, and lazy loading Images were showing "no image" because there was no loading state - users saw nothing while images loaded from WooCommerce. Added: - Animated pulse skeleton placeholder while images load - Auto-retry (2 attempts) for temporarily failed images - Lazy loading for thumbnails, eager loading for main product image - Smooth opacity transition when images finish loading - State reset when image src changes (e.g. switching variants) https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix read-only ref type error in ImageWithFallback Include null in useRef type parameter to make .current mutable. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix ImageWithFallback breaking logo and inline images Remove wrapper div that hijacked className/style from the img element. Images like the logo rely on intrinsic sizing (w-auto, h-10) which broke when className was applied to a parent div instead of the img directly. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix ImageWithFallback: loading skeleton for remote images, plain render for static Static imports (logo, local assets) render as plain without wrapper div or loading skeleton. Remote images (product images from WooCommerce) keep the wrapper div with loading skeleton + retry logic. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix unused preload warning for hero image hidden on mobile Remove priority prop since the hero image container is hidden on screens below lg breakpoint. Also fix sizes hint to 0vw for small screens to avoid downloading a large image that won't be displayed. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix images disappearing on reload due to useEffect race condition The useEffect that resets isLoading on srcUrl change was also firing on initial mount, resetting isLoading back to true after onLoad already set it to false. Now it only resets state when src actually changes, not on the first render. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Rewrite ImageWithFallback: no wrapper div, works for all image types - Static imports (logo): plain , no loading state, className passed through directly so h-10/w-auto/etc work correctly - Remote images (products): plain with opacity fade-in on load, retry on error, className passed through directly so object-cover and sizing classes work correctly - No wrapper
anywhere — eliminates all layout/sizing issues - isLoading starts as false for static images to prevent flash https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h * Fix product images disappearing on page reload Split into two components: static images render plain , remote images use a separate RemoteImage component with proper lifecycle. Key fix: after every render, check imgRef.current.complete to catch images that loaded from browser cache before React could attach the onLoad handler. This was the root cause of images going invisible on reload — cached images fire onLoad before React hydrates. Also replaced key-based remounting with state-driven src updates for retries, preventing component reset issues. https://claude.ai/code/session_01UfkmmZ6PGLmfUX6xbQMH7h --------- Co-authored-by: Claude

docs(#608): Add conversation_browser(summarize) compression recommendations (#609)

**Issue #608:** conversation_browser(summarize) with detailLevel: "NoTools"
generates massive output (309 KB for 23 messages).

**Root cause:**
- NoTools is misleadingly named (masks only tool params, keeps full results)
- truncationChars defaults to 0 (no truncation)

**Solution:** Document recommendations in sddd-conversational-grounding.md:
- Use detailLevel: "Summary" instead of "NoTools" (avoid confusion)
- ALWAYS set truncationChars limit (recommended: 10000)
- Use summarize_type: "trace" for metrics

**Result:** ~3 KB output with proper settings vs ~300 KB with NoTools.

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix unfair bot words and expand letter variety

- Remove emoji words from bot dict statically instead of filtering at runtime

- Fix misleading hint when no valid suggestion exists for current letter
- Expand bot dict with new ending letters (b, k, l, m, o, s, w, y) so player exercises more starting letters

commit.message:
feat: support multi-point lap splitting with equal-distance mode

The split dialog now offers two modes: "Equal split" divides a lap into
N equal-distance segments, and "Manual" picks a single split point.
The underlying splitLap() function accepts multiple indices and splits
right-to-left to keep earlier indices valid. Segment preview shows
colored bars and per-part stats.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Algorithm context-awareness — digital nomad, team building, family rooms, long stay

New trip type detections in generateDestinationShortlist (Claude shortlist):
- Digital nomad: suggests Lisbon, Tbilisi, Chiang Mai, Budapest etc with coworking culture
- Team building: group-friendly cities with meeting/activity infrastructure (max 3h from origin)
- Long stay (≥12 days): cost-of-living guidance, apartment availability, neighbourhood culture
- Family with children: shows estimated children count to Claude, suggests family resorts/apartments
- Friends group: group dynamics guidance for nightlife/gastro/outdoor mix

New trip type detection in optimizeDestination (hotel selection):
- Digital nomad: 65% hotel ratio (value-for-money with workspace) instead of default 70%
- Hotel ratio priority chain: Romantic(85%) > Business(80%) > Family(75%) > Nomad(65%) > Adventure(50%) > Default(70%)

Family room calculation improvements:
- family tripType: infers children from total travelers (assumes 2 adults + rest children)
- totalPeople ≤4 → 1 family room, ≤7 → 2 rooms, 7+ → 3 rooms
- Fixes ternary bug (? 2 : 2 → ? 2 : 3 for large families)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
